label: Heavy going
summary: The world holds at 38 as the US-Iran war grinds through a third night of American strikes and hardens into a blockade. Having declared the ceasefire 'over,' President Trump formally notified Congress that hostilities had resumed and ordered the naval blockade of Iran reinstated as of July 14, with US forces striking dozens more targets and Washington demanding a 20 percent toll — roughly 30 million dollars on a full supertanker — on cargo transiting the Strait of Hormuz. Markets recoiled: oil posted its biggest jump since April with Brent above 83 dollars and WTI near 78, the S&P 500 fell 0.79 percent and the Nasdaq 1.55 percent as chip stocks tumbled, and Tokyo's Nikkei sank more than 4 percent on Japan's exposure to Gulf oil. The one thread of restraint is diplomatic: Trump says negotiators will keep talking, with another US-Iran round expected next week, possibly in Switzerland. Elsewhere the ledger stayed grim. Russia and Ukraine traded drone and missile salvos that killed at least nine, a Latin-culture street festival in Toronto was hit by a shooting that left two dead and several wounded, DR Congo's Bundibugyo-Ebola outbreak passed 700 deaths against nearly 1,950 cases, Gaza's toll climbed past 73,000 amid a still-dire humanitarian picture, and a one-in-1,000-year deluge forced helicopter evacuations in flood-hit southeastern Missouri. Against the gloom, the World Cup semifinals opened with France meeting Spain in Dallas in a last-four drawn entirely from the top-ranked nations, NASA scientists reported a breakthrough on the Sun's tachocline mystery, and an Ebola treatment trial got under way. The headline matches the rubric formula and lands level with yesterday: an active-war Middle East that keeps the day firmly in the 'Bad' band.

drivers.headline: The US-Iran war intensifies as US forces strike Iran for a third straight night and President Trump formally notifies Congress that hostilities have resumed, announcing the reinstatement of a naval blockade of Iran effective July 14; the escalation follows the July 8 collapse of the interim ceasefire, and Iranian state media report retaliatory strikes on US bases across Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan, Oman and Qatar

Oil posts its biggest gain since April after Trump says he will reimpose a blockade on Iranian ships in the Strait of Hormuz and demand a 20 percent reimbursement on all other cargo — roughly 30 million dollars on a full supertanker — as US and Iranian forces exchange strikes; Brent closes above 83 dollars a barrel and WTI trades near 78 on fears for the vital oil chokepoint

Global stocks slide as US-Iran escalation rattles sentiment: the S&P 500 falls 0.79 percent and the tech-heavy Nasdaq drops 1.55 percent as semiconductor names tumble, with Micron off 4 percent and newly listed SK Hynix down 9 percent, while Tokyo's Nikkei sinks more than 4 percent on Japan's heavy dependence on Gulf oil; energy shares cushion the Dow as crude spikes

Russia and Ukraine trade deadly overnight drone and missile salvos that kill at least nine people: Ukrainian strikes leave four dead and seven wounded in Russia's Moscow and Belgorod regions, while Russian attacks kill civilians in Zaporizhzhia and Kherson and strike a Togolese-flagged cargo ship unloading fertiliser, underscoring a war that grinds on with no diplomatic opening in sight

Two men are killed and six other people wounded when a pair of gunmen open fire at Toronto's Salsa on St. Clair festival, a celebration of Latin culture drawing an estimated 13,000 people; police recover two firearms and work three crime scenes, describing an apparent targeted exchange of gunfire, and the festival's final day is cancelled

The Democratic Republic of the Congo's Bundibugyo-virus Ebola outbreak, the fastest-growing on record and now the third largest ever, passes 700 confirmed deaths against nearly 1,950 cases, with Ituri province the epicentre and spread confirmed to North Kivu, South Kivu, Haut-Uele and Tshopo; there is still no approved vaccine or treatment for this strain, and front-line health workers threaten to strike over unpaid wages

A one-in-1,000-year rainfall event dumps 12 inches or more of rain in hours across parts of southeastern Missouri, triggering catastrophic flash flooding that forces the Missouri National Guard to use eight Black Hawk helicopters to evacuate more than 200 children and counsellors stranded at a summer camp, while flood watches and extreme heat blanket a wide swath of the US South, Midwest and West

Nine months into a US-brokered ceasefire, Gaza's death toll climbs past 73,000 as the Security Council prepares another briefing on a humanitarian situation that remains dire; competing assessments clash, with an Israeli COGAT report arguing aid, food and water access improved during the truce even as Palestinian authorities report continued casualties and the sensitive next phase on disarmament and withdrawal stays unimplemented

Pacific civil-society groups condemn China's recent intercontinental ballistic missile test across the ocean — fired about 1,000km northeast of the Solomon Islands hours before Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese was due in Honiara — and urge Pacific leaders to reject missile tests and military expansion, as the region weighs a proposed Australia-Fiji-New Zealand 'Ocean of Peace' defence alignment

Despite declaring the ceasefire over, Trump says US and Iranian negotiators will keep talking, with another round expected the following week, possibly in Switzerland — a slim diplomatic thread running against the escalation, even as he voices little faith that the talks can produce a credible deal

The 2026 World Cup semifinals open with France facing Spain at Dallas Stadium, the first of two last-four ties drawn entirely from the world's top-ranked nations; France arrive unbeaten and yet to concede in the knockouts, while Spain reach their first semifinal since winning the trophy in 2010, with a place in the July 19 final in New Jersey at stake before a global audience

Scientists working with one of NASA's DRIVE Science Centers report a breakthrough on a long-standing solar mystery, moving closer to explaining the extreme thinness of the Sun's tachocline — a hidden layer critical to the star's magnetic dynamo and to the space weather that can disrupt satellites and power grids on Earth

sources_consulted: Wikipedia (2026 Iran war / 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis / 2026 Iran war ceasefire / 2026 Ebola epidemic)
